title: themeda ciliata subsp. helferi
intro: Themeda ciliata subsp. helferi, also known as Helfer's grass, is a subspecies of grass native to Australia. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce swelling and fight off infections. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including relief from fever, coughs, and respiratory issues, as well as potential antioxidant and antiviral effects. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and optimal usage.
